name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-32
title: Chained-workspace topology causes _ensure_branch to silently create orphan branch, overwriting upstream state
description:
When the immediate origin is a local repo that does not have bug-backlog-files in refs/heads/ (only as a remote-tracking ref pointing further upstream), branch_ops.py misbehaves in two ways:

1. Read-side: _branch_exists_on_remote calls 'git ls-remote --heads origin bug-backlog-files'. ls-remote --heads only lists the immediate origin's refs/heads/. In chained topologies (e.g. ws31 -> ws29 -> GitHub) where the intermediate has the branch only as refs/remotes/origin/bug-backlog-files, this returns False even though the branch genuinely exists further up the chain. Operators see misleading 'No items filed yet (branch does not exist)' from list-items.py.

2. Write-side risk: when _branch_exists_on_remote returns False, _ensure_branch calls _init_orphan_branch, which creates a fresh orphan branch and pushes it to the immediate origin. In a chained-workspace setup this would:
   - Create a new bug-backlog-files locally on the intermediate (e.g. ws29) that has no relation to the upstream truth on GitHub
   - Eventually drift / overwrite the legitimate items when someone resolves the divergence

Reproduce:
  1. Set up ws31 with origin = ws29 (local disk).
  2. Ensure ws29 has bug-backlog-files only as a remote-tracking ref, NOT as a local branch (refs/heads/).
  3. From ws31, run any read operation: python3 .claude/features/rabbit-file/scripts/list-items.py --type all
  4. Observe: 'No items filed yet (origin/bug-backlog-files does not exist)' even though it does exist via the chain.
  5. Run any write operation (e.g. file-item.py) — _init_orphan_branch is invoked silently.

Proposed fix (one option): in _ensure_branch, refuse to create an orphan branch when origin appears to be a local repo (file:// or /absolute/path/), and surface a clear error directing the operator to first materialize bug-backlog-files locally in the intermediate. A more ambitious fix would traverse the chain via 'git ls-remote' on the intermediate's own origin if accessible.

Related: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-31 (closed as stale; the originally-described 'missing fetch' was inaccurate).
